mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate held near the zero lower bound (0.08% as of June 2021) suppresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows continuously, producing a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 forward returns as long as the rate remains below its long-run neutral level, independent of any triggering event.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and forward 60-day returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 3-year window, or returns during near-zero rate periods are not statistically higher than the full-sample average (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the unemployment rate signals improving labor market conditions that boost consumer income expectations and corporate earnings outlooks, causing positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days once the decline exceeds a materiality threshold.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE declines by more than 0.2 percentage points month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE=5.9 at 2021-06-01, declining from pandemic peak
failure_condition: Triggered episodes of UNRATE decline > 0.2pp fail to produce positive 20-day forward returns in at least 55% of instances over the 2010-2024 sample, or Rank IC < 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strength in retail sales and housing starts reflects broad-based consumer demand momentum that individually weak single-series signals fail to capture; only when both RSAFS and HOUST rise together does the combined demand impulse translate into positive S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0% AND HOUST month-over-month growth > 0% simultaneously
failure_condition: Joint-positive RSAFS/HOUST episodes do not outperform episodes where only one series is positive by a statistically significant margin (p > 0.05) in forward 20-day returns, or the interaction term shows no incremental Rank IC over either series alone.

mechanism_narrative: During a post-pandemic reopening recovery, sustained acceleration in industrial production reflects supply-side normalization that supports corporate revenue growth and sustains equity market gains over a full quarter horizon; this mechanism is regime-specific to periods of recovering capacity utilization following a demand or supply shock.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO year-over-year growth exceeds 5% during recovery phase
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO=100.0951 vintage 2021-06-01, rising from 2020 pandemic trough
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration above 5% y/y fails to precede positive 60-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances during recovery regimes, or the effect disappears outside recovery periods (regime interaction test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is at or below 0.25% (effective zero lower bound), short-horizon equity returns become highly sensitive to incremental monetary policy signals; a stable near-zero rate reading acts as a short-term risk-on trigger producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as investors price continued accommodation.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns conditional on FEDFUNDS <= 0.25% are not significantly different from unconditional 5-day returns (t-test p > 0.05) over a rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Continuous positive month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls provides an always-on momentum signal for near-term equity market sentiment, as steady employment gains reinforce expectations of consumption and corporate revenue growth, producing modestly positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS=145759 thousand persons vintage 2021-06-01, rising post-2020 trough
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS month-over-month change and forward 5-day returns is below 0.02 over any rolling 3-year window, or sign accuracy does not exceed 52%.

mechanism_narrative: When headline CPI inflation accelerates above a 5% year-over-year threshold, markets price an elevated probability of imminent monetary tightening, compressing valuation multiples and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days until inflation moderates.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL=270.981 vintage 2021-06-01, y/y inflation accelerating sharply in mid-2021
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns during CPI y/y > 5% episodes are not significantly negative relative to baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or positive returns occur in more than 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: The joint condition of near-zero policy rates and elevated unemployment slack signals an extended monetary easing regime consistent with dual-mandate accommodation; only when FEDFUNDS remains near zero AND UNRATE remains above its pre-shock baseline does this combination predict positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon, reflecting sustained policy support through the labor market recovery.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS=0.08 and UNRATE=5.9 as of mid-2021, reflecting easing-with-slack regime
regime_hint: labor market slack combined with easy monetary policy
failure_condition: Joint FEDFUNDS/UNRATE triggered episodes fail to outperform single-factor FEDFUNDS-only or UNRATE-only signals in forward 60-day returns by a statistically significant margin (p > 0.05), indicating no genuine interaction effect.
